title: C: Beginner to Intermediate
summary: A C23-oriented course from first compilation through functions, arrays, pointers, dynamic memory, files, modular builds and defensive systems-level programming.

assumed_knowledge: Programming Foundations or equivalent diagnostic mastery
audience.target_capability: Independently design, build, test and debug a portable multi-file C program while reasoning explicitly about types, object lifetimes, pointers and resource ownership.

includes: C23 core language used by mainstream toolchains
Structures, enums, unions and type aliases
Undefined behavior, diagnostics, sanitizers and tests
non_goals: Advanced C atomics and lock-free design
Compiler extensions presented as portable C

authoritative_sources.title: ISO C Working Group C23 status
GNU C Language Introduction and Reference Manual

runtime.standard: C23 portable subset with deviations labeled
toolchain: GCC or Clang in C23 mode
runtime.execution_environment: Isolated Linux container with warnings enabled and deterministic resource limits
notes: Toolchain-specific extensions are excluded unless explicitly marked.

modules.title: C program lifecycle
modules.description: Create, translate, link and run a minimal C program while reading diagnostics.

skills.title: Translation unit and main
skills.description: Identify declarations, definitions, statements and the hosted program entry point.
outcomes: Write and explain a minimal conforming hosted C program.

skills.title: Preprocess, compile, assemble and link
skills.description: Relate source, object files, libraries and executable output.
outcomes: Diagnose whether a supplied failure occurs during preprocessing, compilation or linking.

skills.title: Warnings and diagnostics
skills.description: Use diagnostic locations and warning categories instead of ignoring warnings.
outcomes: Repair syntax and constraint violations reported by a strict compiler invocation.

skills.title: Library headers and declarations
skills.description: Include the header that declares each standard-library facility and respect its contract.
outcomes: Select required headers and explain the danger of missing or incompatible declarations.

modules.title: Objects, types and expressions
modules.description: Represent values and evaluate expressions with C's type and conversion rules.

skills.title: Scalar types and declarations
skills.description: Use integer, floating, Boolean, character and void-related declarations appropriately.
outcomes: Choose and declare a suitable scalar type while avoiding unjustified size assumptions.

skills.title: Literals and formatted I/O types
skills.description: Match literal forms, suffixes and format specifiers to types.
outcomes: Repair mismatched literals and formatted I/O conversions without invoking undefined behavior.

skills.title: Operators and precedence
skills.description: Use arithmetic, relational, logical, conditional, increment and assignment operators deliberately.
outcomes: Trace expressions and add grouping that makes evaluation intent unambiguous.

skills.title: Conversions and numeric boundaries
skills.description: Reason about promotions, signedness, narrowing, overflow and floating-point approximation.
outcomes: Predict and test conversion results while guarding values that cannot be represented.

modules.title: Control flow and input/output
modules.description: Build robust console programs using selection, iteration and validated input.

skills.title: if and switch
skills.description: Express decisions with Boolean conditions and integral dispatch.
outcomes: Implement and test boundary-complete if and switch logic.

skills.title: while, do and for
skills.description: Choose and implement loops with explicit progress and termination.
outcomes: Write and debug counted, sentinel and input-driven loops.

skills.title: break, continue and return
skills.description: Use structured early exits without obscuring invariants or cleanup.
outcomes: Refactor loop and function exits for correct behavior and readable control flow.

skills.title: Validated text input
skills.description: Read bounded text, parse values and handle malformed or exhausted input.
outcomes: Implement a console input loop that rejects malformed and out-of-range values safely.

modules.title: Functions, scope and recursion
modules.description: Design typed interfaces and reason about calls, names and storage duration.

skills.title: Declarations, prototypes and definitions
skills.description: Declare functions before use and keep interfaces compatible with definitions.
outcomes: Design, declare, define and call a type-correct function.

skills.title: Parameters and return values
skills.description: Apply C's value-passing model and use pointer parameters only with clear contracts.
outcomes: Predict which caller-visible values change across a function call.

skills.title: Scope, linkage and storage duration
skills.description: Distinguish block/file scope, internal/external linkage and automatic/static duration.
outcomes: Resolve name visibility and lifetime in a multi-function example.

skills.title: Recursive functions
skills.description: Implement terminating recursion while accounting for call-stack cost.
outcomes: Write, trace and test a recursive function with explicit base and progress cases.

modules.title: Arrays and strings
modules.description: Store and process contiguous fixed-size data without violating bounds.

skills.title: One-dimensional arrays
skills.description: Declare, initialize, measure in scope and traverse arrays within valid bounds.
outcomes: Implement and test array traversal, update and aggregation routines.

skills.title: Multidimensional arrays
skills.description: Use row-major nested arrays and pass dimensions correctly to functions.
outcomes: Implement a bounded two-dimensional grid traversal and explain its layout.

skills.title: C string representation
skills.description: Treat strings as character arrays terminated by a null character with finite capacity.
outcomes: Trace string length, storage capacity and termination in memory.

skills.title: Bounded string processing
skills.description: Copy, compare, search and parse text while tracking capacities and failure cases.
outcomes: Implement a bounded string-processing routine and tests for empty, full and malformed inputs.

modules.title: Pointers and indirection
modules.description: Use addresses and pointer expressions with explicit validity and lifetime reasoning.

skills.title: Addresses and dereference
skills.description: Create, inspect and dereference pointers only while they designate valid objects.
outcomes: Trace pointer and pointee state and identify null, invalid and dangling cases.

skills.title: Pointers, arrays and arithmetic
skills.description: Relate array conversion, indexing and pointer arithmetic within one array object.
outcomes: Rewrite and trace equivalent bounded array and pointer traversals.

skills.title: const-correct pointer interfaces
skills.description: Distinguish pointer constness from pointee constness and express read-only contracts.
outcomes: Design a pointer-based function signature with the least required mutation rights.

skills.title: Function pointers and callbacks
skills.description: Store compatible function addresses and invoke callbacks through typed interfaces.
outcomes: Implement and explain a small callback-based operation without incompatible casts.

modules.title: Structured and named types
modules.description: Model domain data using structures, enumerations, unions and aliases.

skills.title: Structures
skills.description: Define, initialize, copy and access related named fields.
outcomes: Model and process a small domain entity using a structure and functions.

skills.title: Enumerations
skills.description: Represent a closed set of named integral states and validate external values.
outcomes: Use an enum with exhaustive control flow and a safe invalid-value policy.

skills.title: Unions and tagged values
skills.description: Understand shared storage and pair a union with a valid discriminant.
outcomes: Implement and safely inspect a simple tagged union.

skills.title: Type aliases and API clarity
skills.description: Use typedef to name existing types without confusing aliases with new runtime representations.
outcomes: Create readable public type names while preserving const and pointer meaning.

modules.title: Dynamic memory and files
modules.description: Acquire, validate, use and release memory and file resources on every path.

skills.title: Object lifetime and ownership
skills.description: Relate automatic, static and allocated objects to valid reference lifetimes.
outcomes: Identify owner, borrowers and release point for each resource in a routine.

skills.title: Allocation and deallocation
skills.description: Use malloc, calloc, realloc and free with overflow and failure checks.
outcomes: Implement a dynamically sized array without leak, double-free or lost-pointer errors.

skills.title: File streams and binary/text modes
skills.description: Open, read, write, check and close streams with clear ownership.
outcomes: Implement text file persistence that handles open, parse, I/O and close failures.

skills.title: Failure-safe cleanup
skills.description: Structure cleanup so partially acquired resources are released exactly once.
outcomes: Repair a multi-resource function to preserve the first failure and release all acquired resources.

modules.title: Modular and defensive C
modules.description: Build maintainable multi-file programs and verify behavior with tools and tests.

skills.title: Header interfaces and include guards
skills.description: Separate public declarations from private definitions without duplicate-definition hazards.
outcomes: Create a self-contained header and matching implementation for a small module.

skills.title: Preprocessor and macros
skills.description: Use conditional compilation and simple macros while avoiding multiple evaluation and hidden control flow.
outcomes: Replace unsafe macro patterns and explain preprocessing results.

skills.title: Multi-file builds
skills.description: Compile separate translation units and link reproducibly with declared dependencies.
outcomes: Build and diagnose a multi-file command-line program using a repeatable build description.

skills.title: Undefined behavior, tests and sanitizers
skills.description: Distinguish defined, implementation-defined and undefined behavior and combine tests with dynamic diagnostics.
outcomes: Find and repair bounds, lifetime, overflow and uninitialized-state defects using tests and sanitizers.

exit_outcomes: Build and diagnose a portable multi-file C program with strict warnings and a repeatable build.
Use arrays, structures, pointers, dynamic memory and files while explaining bounds, lifetimes and ownership.
Design typed module interfaces and test normal, boundary, malformed-input and allocation-failure paths.
Use sanitizer evidence and the C behavioral model to identify and remove undefined behavior.
